contents.value:
```typescript
enum Name { ... }
```
Defines a set of named constants. TypeScript supports numeric and string enums.

```typescript
enum Status { Active = 'ACTIVE', Inactive = 'INACTIVE' }
enum Direction { Up, Down, Left, Right } // 0, 1, 2, 3
```

**Tip:** Use `const enum` for enums that should be fully erased at compile time.

```typescript
type Record<K extends keyof any, T> = { [P in K]: T }
```
Constructs an object type whose property keys are `K` and values are `T`.

Useful for dictionaries and lookup objects.

```typescript
type Partial<T> = { [P in keyof T]?: T[P] }
```
Makes all properties in `T` optional. Commonly used for update/patch operations.

```typescript
type Required<T> = { [P in keyof T]-?: T[P] }
```
Makes all properties in `T` required (removes optional `?` modifier).

```typescript
type Readonly<T> = { readonly [P in keyof T]: T[P] }
```
Makes all properties in `T` readonly — assignments will cause a compile-time error.

```typescript
type Pick<T, K extends keyof T> = { [P in K]: T[P] }
```
Constructs a type by picking the set of properties `K` from `T`.

```typescript
type Omit<T, K extends keyof any> = Pick<T, Exclude<keyof T, K>>
```
Constructs a type by removing the set of properties `K` from `T`.

```typescript
type Exclude<T, U> = T extends U ? never : T
```
Excludes from `T` those types that are assignable to `U`.

```typescript
type Extract<T, U> = T extends U ? T : never
```
Extracts from `T` those types that are assignable to `U`.

```typescript
type NonNullable<T> = T & {}
```
Excludes `null` and `undefined` from `T`.

```typescript
type ReturnType<T extends (...args: any) => any> = T extends (...args: any) => infer R ? R : any
```
Obtains the return type of a function type `T`.

```typescript
type Parameters<T extends (...args: any) => any> = T extends (...args: infer P) => any ? P : never
```
Obtains the parameter types of a function type `T` as a tuple.

```typescript
type Awaited<T> = T extends PromiseLike<infer U> ? Awaited<U> : T
```
Recursively unwraps the `Awaited` type of `Promise`-like types. Added in TypeScript 4.5.

```typescript
keyof Type
```
The `keyof` operator takes an object type and produces a string or numeric literal union of its keys.

```typescript
type Point = { x: number; y: number };
type P = keyof Point; // 'x' | 'y'
```

```typescript
expression as const
```
Const assertion — infers the narrowest/most specific type possible. Array literals become `readonly` tuples, object literals get `readonly` properties, and string/number literals are not widened.

```typescript
const arr = [1, 2, 3] as const; // readonly [1, 2, 3]
```

```typescript
expression satisfies Type
```
*TypeScript 4.9+* — Validates that an expression's type matches `Type` without changing the inferred type. Useful for catching errors while preserving narrow types.

```typescript
T extends SomeType<infer U> ? U : Default
```
The `infer` keyword is used within conditional types to declaratively introduce a new generic type variable to be inferred by TypeScript.

```typescript
type never
```
The `never` type represents the type of values that never occur. Used for:
- Functions that never return (throw or infinite loop)
- Exhaustive checks in switch/if statements
- The empty union type (impossible values)

```typescript
type unknown
```
The type-safe counterpart of `any`. A value of type `unknown` cannot be used until it is narrowed via type guards, assertions, or control flow analysis.

Prefer `unknown` over `any` for values of uncertain type.

```typescript
type NoInfer<T> = intrinsic
```
*TypeScript 5.4+* — Blocks inference from flowing to this type position. Forces TypeScript to infer `T` from other positions.

An **HTML element** consists of a start tag, content, and end tag.
```html
<tag>content</tag>
```

An **attribute** provides additional information about an element.
```html
<tag attr="value">
```

A **CSS selector** targets HTML elements for styling.
```css
.class, #id, tag { property: value; }
```

An **event** fires when user interaction or browser activity occurs.
```
click, keydown, load, submit, resize
```
The **Document Object Model** represents the HTML page as a tree of nodes.

The **viewport** is the visible area of a web page in the browser window.
```html
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width">
```
**Responsive design** adapts layouts to different screen sizes using media queries and flexible units.

```typescript
type ConstructorParameters<T extends abstract new (...args: any) => any> = T extends abstract new (...args: infer P) => any ? P : never
```
Extracts parameter types from a constructor function type.

```typescript
type InstanceType<T extends abstract new (...args: any) => any> = T extends abstract new (...args: any) => infer R ? R : any
```
Constructs the instance type of a constructor function type.

```typescript
type ThisType<T> = T
```
Marker for contextual this type. Only works with --noImplicitThis.

```typescript
type Uppercase<S extends string> = intrinsic
```
Converts string literal type to uppercase.

```typescript
type Lowercase<S extends string> = intrinsic
```
Converts string literal type to lowercase.

```typescript
type Capitalize<S extends string> = intrinsic
```
Capitalizes first character of string literal type.

```typescript
type Uncapitalize<S extends string> = intrinsic
```
Uncapitalizes first character of string literal type.

```typescript
interface ReadonlyArray<T>
```
An array that cannot be mutated. No push, pop, splice, etc.

```typescript
interface Promise<T>
```
Represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation. Inherits all JavaScript Promise features with full type safety.

```typescript
interface Disposable { [Symbol.dispose](): void }
```
TypeScript 5.2+ — Interface for the using declaration. Implement Symbol.dispose for automatic cleanup.

```typescript
interface AsyncDisposable { [Symbol.asyncDispose](): PromiseLike<void> }
```
TypeScript 5.2+ — Interface for the await using declaration. Implement Symbol.asyncDispose for async resource cleanup.
